question: q01
statement: Develop a model that provides a measure of the ability of a region to provide clean water to meet the needs of its population. You may need to consider the dynamic nature of the factors that affect both supply and demand.
methods: 读取 World Bank Jordan 缓存指标，用 freshwater withdrawals/internal renewable resources 构造物理水压力，并用基础饮水、卫生服务和 GDP/capita 修正经济稀缺。对应模型：水资源压力指数、系统动力学、多指标评价。

data_source.root: docs/mcm-2015-2025/official_assets_extracted/2016/Problem Data- Are we heading towards a thirsty planet
data_source.source_pdf: docs/mcm-2015-2025/official_assets_extracted/2016/Are we heading towards a thirsty planet.pdf

indicators.ER.H2O.FWTL.ZS: Annual freshwater withdrawals, total (% of internal resources)
indicators.ER.H2O.FWAG.ZS: Annual freshwater withdrawals, agriculture (% of total freshwater withdrawal)
indicators.ER.H2O.FWIN.ZS: Annual freshwater withdrawals, industry (% of total freshwater withdrawal)
indicators.ER.H2O.FWDM.ZS: Annual freshwater withdrawals, domestic (% of total freshwater withdrawal)
indicators.SH.H2O.BASW.ZS: People using at least basic drinking water services
indicators.SH.STA.BASS.ZS: People using at least basic sanitation services

water_scarcity_model.definition: A region is critical when withdrawals exceed internal renewable resources after adjusting for service gaps and economic capacity.

ER.H2O.FWTL.ZS.indicator_name: Annual freshwater withdrawals, total (% of internal resources)

ER.H2O.FWAG.ZS.indicator_name: Annual freshwater withdrawals, agriculture (% of total freshwater withdrawal)

ER.H2O.FWIN.ZS.indicator_name: Annual freshwater withdrawals, industry (% of total freshwater withdrawal)

ER.H2O.FWDM.ZS.indicator_name: Annual freshwater withdrawals, domestic (% of total freshwater withdrawal)

SH.H2O.BASW.ZS.indicator_name: People using at least basic drinking water services

SH.STA.BASS.ZS.indicator_name: People using at least basic sanitation services

components.interpretation: economic scarcity proxy through sanitation service gap

drivers.physical_scarcity: freshwater withdrawals exceed internal renewable resources
drivers.economic_scarcity: near-universal basic water access hides infrastructure leakage, sanitation, cost, and reliability gaps
drivers.sector_driver: agriculture remains the largest withdrawal share in the cached World Bank observations

assumption_audit.truthfulness_note: COMAP supplied the PDF task statement and named water-resource data sources; this workflow uses cached World Bank observations for Jordan plus explicit planning assumptions for interventions.

assumptions.region_choice_note: Jordan is selected as a heavily overloaded water-scarce region; the water stress indicator is observed in World Bank data.
assumptions.baseline_model: stress index = freshwater withdrawals as percent of internal renewable resources divided by 100; values above 1 mean withdrawals exceed internal resources.
assumptions.no_intervention_demand_growth: future stress grows with recent population trend multiplied by the problem statement claim that water use has grown at twice the population rate.
assumptions.intervention_effect_note: intervention effects are transparent planning assumptions applied to observed baseline indicators, not historical causal estimates.
